author: Jamie King | Minutes Network
msg:
Unity Verified - quick update for node holders:

A naming issue is currently affecting how the Unity Network app appears in some Google Play Stores.

We’re actively investigating and working with the relevant parties to resolve this as quickly as possible. A potential rename may be required.

What this means for you:

The network continues to run as normal
Your node activity and participation are unaffected
No impact to your data or progress
The APK remains fully available and unaffected

This is a surface-level issue only, and it’s being handled.

We’ll keep this group updated first as things progress - appreciate your continued support.

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg:
Additional procedure to extract 1,000 records from the manage.unitynodes.io page (after the website update it extracts only the first 20 by default)

1. Open DevTools (F12) → Go to Network tab.
2. Reload the page and find the request named rewards_get_allocations.
3. Right-click on that request row → Copy → Copy as fetch.
4. Go to Console tab.
5. Paste the code.
6. If Chrome says “Paste is not allowed”, type allow pasting, press Enter and paste the text again.
7. Make these two changes only:
    Change "take": 20 → "take": 1000
    Change "credentials": "include" → "credentials": "same-origin"

8. Press Enter.
9. Wait a few seconds → Move back to Network tab and copy the results that include 1,000 records from the  rewards_get_allocations which is bigger in size, look for 90kb or so

author: L .
msg:
I’ve been using the “email@gmail.com, email+1@gmail.com, email+2@gmail.com” method.  Then I numbered my self ran phones & recorded what number goes to with each email.

That way all emails sent go to the same inbox & you can tell what phone/license the email or log in is for based on the email address it was sent to.

Easy to keep track of, you only have to log into “email@gmail.com” to see all emails sent to any of the “email+X@gmail.com” addresses.  

Hope that makes sense…😎

author: Calus B
msg: This is what I am running too

author: Calus B
msg: It’s important to actually make sure you number your phones though

author: Calus B
msg: lol

author: L .
msg:
ABSOLUTELY!  I was definitely meticulous(& redundant at times) in my organization.  

Worth it tho, as everything is super easy to keep track of now.

author: Quoia75
msg:
__But I generally don't see much utility, WM might be one of few projects that I saw real utility and if I ommit this painful slow start for Unity, WM is making me money back as promised.__

Lot's of utility (and potential) with Cardano - but you illustrate its biggest obstacle perfectly: devs are not marketers. If Cardano had implemented a strong marketing component in first 2-3 years of life it would be in a stronger adoption position IMHO, and more people would know what it's for and and it's value proposition.

The governance model by its nature is high risk - and it certainly hasn't been a perfect ride, but people are putting in the effort to achieve the chain's core mission - decentralization, and that deserves some respect. The world is in desperate need of a working model of what decentralized governance looks like, so let them build it. I hope it survives. I'm a DRep over there, so I do my  part as I can.

But I get what you're saying - if your interest in blockchain & crypto is purely an investment-and-financial-return play, there are faster ways to reach that goal. Here's hoping the Watkins have figured it out!

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Yeah, reported it as well. I suspect that during the process of opting in for the CLI task you click "accept" before entering the phone number. Then, somehow, the phone number gets send and since you already accpted the terms you get put in the pool of numbers that can be called. However, since it doesnt register on your end your CLI task isnt live so you cant opt out to get your number removed from the pool.

I also have the theory that maybe the issue is if you have more than one phone number associated with the same account/emailaddress/walletaddress. Because my first phone, that is tied to the same email address, has been verified months ago.

author: Jamie King | Minutes Network
msg:
Hi everyone - quick update on the Scout & Runner MapQuest public launch.

We know there’s a lot of excitement around this release - and we’re just as excited to get it into your hands.

We’re pushing the launch back slightly whilst we complete some final polish. The team is actively on it, and we want to make sure everything is in the right place before launch.

It’s a short delay, but the right one.

Thanks for your patience

author: DZ
msg:
What is the formula the Unity team came up with a $19,200 potential for a node holder? 

For me; prior to purchasing my first node. I came up with the idea I could use all my 200 licenses on the 200 phones I would buy (phone farm) and with earning $96 each per month would give me that exact number of the 19,200. 

Now after 6 months that seems impossible, improbable, and was never meant to be. I just wonder what other method the team has to still be using that number on the Unity Website?

That's ONLY 2 tasks, imagine when we have 10....20....30 tasks
